estimate_the_r_for_this_scatter_plot: Estimate the R² for this scatter plot
scatter_for_relationships_line_for_tim: Scatter for relationships, line for time, bar for categories, histogram for distribution shape, box plot for spread + outliers, ogive for cumulative. Choosing the wrong chart type usually misleads more than it informs.
mean_vs_median_median_wins_for_skewed_: Mean vs median (median wins for skewed data), standard deviation vs IQR (IQR wins for non-normal), correlation vs causation (low correlation does NOT prove no causal link).
ap_stats_data_literacy_items_each_ques: AP Stats / data-literacy items. Each question links back to the active dataset so you can verify visually before answering.
paste_from_google_sheets_generate_samp: Paste from Google Sheets, generate sample data, export as PNG/CSV. Most students underestimate how much real-world data analysis is data CLEANING (~70% of total time).
set_a_true_population_r_then_watch_how: Set a true population r, then watch how sample size, noise, and outliers distort the observed r. No score, no reveal — form a hypothesis and explore.
x_coordinate_for_new_data_point: X coordinate for new data point
y_coordinate_for_new_data_point: Y coordinate for new data point
sample_size_interpret_r_with_n_in_mind: Sample size — interpret R² with n in mind
add_data_points_to_see_statistics: Add data points to see statistics
select_a_quiz_type_and_click_start_qui: Select a quiz type and click "Start Quiz"!
transformations_modify_data_in_place_u: 💡 Transformations modify data in-place. Use Undo (↩) to revert.
generate_20_points_with_a_target_corre: Generate 20 points with a target correlation strength:
watch_how_statistics_change_as_each_po: Watch how statistics change as each point is revealed one-by-one.
observed_r_matches_the_true_population: Observed r matches the true population r within 0.05.
observed_r_is_off_by_0_05_0_15_expecte: Observed r is off by 0.05-0.15. Expected for small n or moderate noise.
observed_r_differs_by_0_15_0_30_a_real: Observed r differs by 0.15-0.30. A real effect can look weak (or vice versa).
observed_r_is_wildly_off_0_30_outliers: Observed r is wildly off (>0.30). Outliers or tiny n can completely flip apparent direction.
set_true_population_r_sample_size_nois: Set true population r, sample size, noise, outliers. Predict how far observed r drifts. No score, no reveal.
your_hypothesis_which_single_change_mo: Your hypothesis (which single change most distorts the observed r?)
e_g_2_outliers_at_n_20_do_more_damage_: e.g., 2 outliers at n=20 do more damage than 10 outliers at n=200...
can_outliers_flip_the_sign_of_r_try_tr: Can outliers FLIP the sign of r? Try true r=+0.7 with 3 outliers.
how_does_increasing_n_reduce_the_noisy: How does increasing n reduce the noisy-estimate state?
when_does_noise_sd_swamp_the_true_sign: When does noise SD swamp the true signal entirely - what does r look like then?
why_is_r_alone_never_enough_to_publish: Why is r alone never enough to publish? What else should accompany it (CI, scatter, robustness check)?
i_can_explain_why_this_combination_of_: I can explain why this combination of n, noise, and outliers produces this divergence.
inquiry_widget_no_score_no_reveal_no_a: Inquiry widget - no score, no reveal, no answer dump. Synthetic data uses a deterministic seed from sliders, so the same slider position always reproduces the same draw. Real studies see one draw and have to reason about uncertainty.
